nodes.title: Prompting LTX-2
widgets_values:
## LTX-2 Prompting Tips
1. **Core Actions**: Describe events and actions as they occur over time  
2. **Audio**: Describe sounds and dialogue needed for the scene  
3. **Reference Image**: Do not repeat details already present  
4. **Consistency**: Avoid instructions that do not match the reference image, as this will degrade results

nodes.title: About  Models
widgets_values:
Download models from here:


https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy

Text encoder : https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/ltx-2

nodes.title: Prompt Enhancer (Optional)
widgets_values: This automagically enhances your prompt using the already loaded Gemma model. But it can be a bit sensitive to having correct Gemma if using GGUF models. Alternatively you can bypass/disable  this feature

nodes.title: Sampler Preview
widgets_values:
Tiny VAE for sampler previews. 
Optionally you can drop this for a lower res latent RGB built into the node 

https://github.com/madebyollin/taehv/blob/main/safetensors/taeltx2_3.safetensors

nodes.title: Distilled lora (Only if using ltx DEV model)
Negative Audio Prompt
widgets_values: distorted sound, saturated sound, loud sound

text, subtitles, logo, low quality, distorted, bad anatomy, oversaturated, pixelated, low resolution, grainy, compression artifacts, jpeg artifacts, glitches, watermark, signature, copyright,  distortedsound, saturated sound, loud sound , deformed facial features, asymmetrical face, missing facial features, extra limbs, disfigured hands, blurry teeth, disfigured teeth

nodes.title: User Made Loras
widgets_values: If using some user made LTX-2 loras they sometimes are not trained on audio, so it will produce very noisy audio outputs. Try use KJNodes LTX-2 Lora Loader Advanced in such cases, and set the non video strenght to zero

nodes.title: PROMPT
widgets_values:
The Joker looks at the camera and talks, he says "OH i take a little LTX break right now". 

Then he laughs.

nodes.title: LONG VIDEO INPUTS
widgets_values:
You can adjust where the video should load from using the **skip_first_frames** setting. But make sure you leave about 3 second or more before your retake starts.  

The same with **frame_load_cap** to set max loaded length, and also keep this to about 3 second or more after your retake ends

nodes.title: Typical normalize audio values
widgets_values:
YouTube / Spotify etc : -14 LUFS

Podcasts: -16 LUFS

Instagram/TikTok: -12 to -10 LUFS

Apple Music: -16 LUFS

TV/Broadcast (US): -24 LUFS

nodes.title: ReTake Video settings
widgets_values:
**MAX SIZE**:  Max pixels of the output video (longest side). If lower ram/vram try reasonable sizes such as 768,  832 or 960. And if the original video is lower than that, set same as original video 

**RETAKE START**:  Set the seconds where you want to start recreating your video 

**RETAKE END**:  Set the seconds where you want the retake to end 

**THIS WORKFLOW IS MADE TO EDIT A SECTION OF YOUR INPUT VIDEO, SO RETAKE START AND END MUST BE WITHIN THE VIDEO LENGTH**

nodes.title: FOR SINGLE PASS TURN OFF BOTH TOGGLES

groups.title: Peformace tweaks (may help low vram etc)

nodes.title: Enhanced Prompt
widgets_values: The Joker stares directly into the camera, his face smeared with white and red makeup, a sinister grin stretching across his lips. He speaks in a low, unsettling voice, "OH i take a little LTX break right now." A chilling, maniacal laugh erupts from him, echoing with a disturbing intensity.

inputs.text: A medium close-up shot features a Caucasian man with a closely shaven head and face, wearing a black baseball cap with "PNTR" in white letters on the front, and a dark grey t-shirt with "JUST DO IT" visible across his chest. A small black microphone is clipped to his shirt collar. He is positioned slightly to the left of the frame, looking intently downwards and to his right, his eyes focused off-camera. His facial expression is one of deep concentration, with his brow slightly furrowed. As he looks down, a quick sniff sound is heard, and then he speaks with a deep male voice and a slightly frustrated tone, saying, "I think it's so bad." The camera remains static throughout, maintaining a shallow depth of field, which keeps the man in sharp focus while the background is softly blurred, revealing a light-colored wall with white wooden shelving or trim, and a partially open white wooden door on the right. After a brief pause, another short, audible sniff is heard. The man then continues to speak, his voice maintaining the same quality, as he states, "So bad. So bad." He elaborates further, emphasizing his point with a final statement, "This got to be, it's got to be the worst tool I've ever seen."
